sections.heading: Surgery
sections.content:
Surgical resection is a component of curative-intent treatment for localized disease and for oligometastatic disease with resectable lesions in patients with good performance status. It is also used for tissue diagnosis.
Surgical resection is considered for isolated, resectable metastases (e.g., liver, lung, bone with impending fracture) in patients with good PS.
Lymph node dissection is recommended for localized nodal disease (e.g., axillary, inguinal).
Surgery for impending fracture is recommended for bone metastases in weight-bearing areas in patients with good PS.
Surgical management of brain metastases follows NCCN Guidelines for Central Nervous System Cancers.
Resectability is determined by the ability to achieve a complete (R0) resection with acceptable morbidity and in patients with adequate performance status. For liver metastases, factors include number, size, and location of lesions, as well as sufficient future liver reserve. For lung nodules, complete resectability is key. For bone, surgery is for stabilization of impending fracture rather than oncologic resection.
Optimization of performance status, nutritional status, and management of comorbidities is critical. For liver surgery, assessment of hepatic functional reserve is essential. For bone surgery, optimization of pain control and stabilization.

cards.name: Lymph node dissection
cards.detail:
approach: Surgical excision of involved lymph node basin (axillary, inguinal).
indications: Localized adenocarcinoma or SCC involving axillary or inguinal lymph nodes.
key_complications: Lymphedema, wound complications, nerve damage.
operative_mortality: Not specified in source text.

cards.name: Surgical resection of metastasis
cards.detail:
approach: Resection of isolated metastatic lesion (e.g., liver, lung).
indications: Solitary or limited metastases in patients with good PS and no evidence of disseminated disease.
key_complications: Organ-specific surgical risks (e.g., bleeding, infection, organ dysfunction).
operative_mortality: Not specified in source text.

cards.name: Surgery for impending fracture
cards.detail:
approach: Orthopedic fixation or stabilization of bone at risk of pathologic fracture.
indications: Bone metastasis with potential for fracture in a weight-bearing area in patients with good PS.
key_complications: Surgical site infection, hardware failure, persistent pain.
operative_mortality: Not specified in source text.
